name: Karekare
notes: Karekare (Karai-karai) — West Chadic (Afroasiatic) language of northeastern Nigeria. Written in the Latin script. GRAPHEME INVENTORY NOT YET SPECIFIED: Karekare is a low-resource language with no widely codified orthography and no reliably published letter→phoneme table; it is also tonal, which any future spec must model. INPUT CONTRACT: input is expected in the Latin script. NOTE: no ISO 639-3 code or Glottolog code is linked from the Wikidata item for the Karai-karai Wikipedia language code (kai); the Karekare language itself is ISO 639-3 kai in Ethnologue, but this spec records only what is verified upstream. wikidata_qid left unset: the code's Wikidata row resolves to the Wikimedia project item, not the language item; not recorded rather than record a wrong id.
